title: 2,561 Strangers Saw the Same Reality Code on DMT. Are We Living in a Simulation?
title_es: 2,561 desconocidos vieron el mismo código de la realidad bajo DMT. ¿Vivimos en una simulación?
description: On three continents, in four decades, on a drug whose entire experience lasts seven minutes, strangers describe the same thing: kanji-like glyphs scrolling vertically through space. Two lasers crossed in front of closed eyes reportedly sharpen them. Johns Hopkins surveyed 2,561 of those strangers. Imperial College mapped their brains. The data doesn't fit the official story — and it isn't going anywhere. If you watched The Matrix, if you suspect there are things they aren't telling you, or if you're optimizing your nervous system, this is the file we built for you.
description_es: En tres continentes, en cuatro décadas, sobre una droga cuya experiencia entera dura siete minutos, desconocidos describen lo mismo: glifos tipo kanji desplazándose verticalmente por el espacio. Dos láseres cruzados frente a los ojos cerrados, según los reportes, los afilan. Johns Hopkins encuestó a 2,561 de esos desconocidos. Imperial College mapeó sus cerebros. La información no encaja con la historia oficial — y no se va a ningún lado. Si viste The Matrix, si sospechas que hay cosas que no te están contando, o si estás optimizando tu sistema nervioso, este es el archivo que construimos para ti.
author: Alejandro del Palacio
keywords: DMT entity encounter survey Johns Hopkins
DMTx extended state DMT Imperial College
personas: consciousness biohacker (Huberman/Hof crowd, nervous system optimizers)
imageAlt: Two crossed laser beams cutting through dark space, intersecting in front of a silhouetted figure as a cascade of luminous glyph-like characters streams down between them
imagePrompt: Retrofuturist 1970s cinematic still photograph. Pitch-black laboratory chamber dominates 70 percent of the frame in deep matte black with heavy 35mm grain. Two narrow saturated laser beams — one electric violet, one neon emerald — cross at sharp angles in midair, intersecting at eye level. Between the beams: a vertical cascade of luminous glyph-like characters streaming downward in chromatic green and bone white, resembling abstract Japanese-influenced sigils, kanji, and code fragments — never literal kanji, always invented glyphs. Small back-turned silhouette of a figure in dark coat stands at the foreground observing the convergence. Vintage analog control rack with faint amber LED indicators in deep shadow. The lasers and glyph cascade are the ONLY chromatic zone — vibrant violet, emerald, and bone-white code breaking out of the dark matrix. Heavy cinematic 35mm film grain, anamorphic lens flare, VHS scanline overlay, kodak ektachrome retro slide aesthetic. Mood: Tron 1982 + Tarkovsky Stalker + The Matrix 1999 green-rain falling code + Apple 1984. The dark room is the matrix; the cascading glyphs between the lasers are the convergence multiple strangers report. No recognizable face. Mysterious retrofuturist forensic. Photographic NOT painterly NOT illustrated.
researchSources: Strassman, R. (2001). DMT: The Spirit Molecule. Park Street Press. UNM clinical trials 1990-1995.
Timmermann, C. et al. (2018). DMT Models the Near-Death Experience. Frontiers in Psychology.
Timmermann, C. et al. (2019). Neural correlates of the DMT experience assessed with multivariate EEG. Scientific Reports 9, 16324.
Carhart-Harris, R. L. et al. (2014). The entropic brain. Frontiers in Human Neuroscience.
Davis, A. K. et al. (2020). Survey of entity encounter experiences occasioned by inhaled DMT. Journal of Psychopharmacology, 34(9).
Lawrence, D. W. et al. (2022). Phenomenology and content of the inhaled DMT experience. Scientific Reports.
Klüver, H. (1928, 1966). Mescal and Mechanisms of Hallucinations. Form-constant doctrine.
Bressloff, P. C. et al. (2001). Geometric visual hallucinations, Euclidean symmetry and the functional architecture of striate cortex. Phil Trans R Soc B.
Gallimore, A. R. (2018). Alien Information Theory. Strange Worlds Press.
Slattery, D. R. (2010). Xenolinguistics: Psychedelics, Language, and the Evolution of Consciousness.
seoTitle: DMT Laser Experiment: Johns Hopkins Survey + Imperial EEG (2026)
seoTitle_es: Experimento láser DMT: encuesta Johns Hopkins + EEG Imperial (2026)
seoDescription: Johns Hopkins surveyed 2,561 DMT users; many describe identical kanji-like glyphs. Imperial College's EEG mapped the brain state. Coincidence or simulation?
seoDescription_es: Johns Hopkins encuestó a 2,561 usuarios de DMT; muchos describen glifos idénticos tipo kanji. El EEG de Imperial College mapeó el estado cerebral. ¿Coincidencia o simulación?
bluf: On three continents over four decades, 2,561 DMT users (Johns Hopkins survey, Davis et al. 2020) independently described the same visual: kanji-like glyphs scrolling vertically through space. Imperial College London brain-scan studies (Timmermann et al. 2019) mapped measurable neural correlates. Two crossed lasers in front of closed eyes reportedly sharpen the imagery. The phenomenology is shockingly consistent across cultures and decades. The mechanism remains unknown.
bluf_es: En tres continentes durante cuatro décadas, 2,561 usuarios de DMT (encuesta de Johns Hopkins, Davis et al. 2020) describieron independientemente la misma visión: glifos similares a kanji desplazándose verticalmente por el espacio. Estudios de imagen cerebral en Imperial College Londres (Timmermann et al. 2019) mapearon correlatos neuronales medibles. Dos láseres cruzados frente a los ojos cerrados supuestamente afilan la imaginería. La fenomenología es sorprendentemente consistente entre culturas y décadas. El mecanismo sigue siendo desconocido.
